text: 0.4g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, stable carbs plus protein is the dream.
14.5g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
5.4g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
vitamin c at 44% daily value stands out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

pros first: does a lot of jobs at once. protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable. fibre-forward category identity. legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre. filling for the calories. legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety.

on the weaker side: can take planning or prep. that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier. dry legumes especially ask more from the cook. can be rough on digestion for some people. practical tolerance still matters.
B tier. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
